entry.name: v-click-outside
entry.description_fallback: Calls a handler when a click lands outside the bound element. Supports Shadow DOM via handleShadow() and an optional closeConditional guard to suppress the callback.
entry.note_fallback: The directive attaches listeners in capture phase on the document (or the nearest Shadow Root) so it intercepts clicks before any element handler can stop propagation.

directiveArgs.name: handler
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Function called when a click lands outside the element. Also accepts the full options object in place of a bare function.

directiveArgs.name: closeConditional
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Optional guard. The handler fires only when this function returns true. Useful to suppress callbacks while a panel is already closed.

directiveArgs.name: include
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Returns a list of elements that should be treated as part of the component. Clicks on these elements do not trigger the handler.

relations.description_fallback: Floating menu that uses v-click-outside internally to close on outside click.
Modal dialog that leverages v-click-outside when persistent=false.

entry.name: v-contrast
entry.description_fallback: Automatically adjusts foreground text colour to meet the WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 by default, configurable via createOrigam). Pass false to opt out on a specific element.
entry.note_fallback: The threshold and algorithm are set globally in createOrigam({ contrast: { threshold: 4.5 } }). Individual elements can override with v-contrast="false" but cannot raise the threshold above the global value.

examples.code:
<template>
  <!-- Text colour is computed to satisfy WCAG AA against the background -->
  <origam-btn v-contrast color="primary">
    Auto-legible text
  </origam-btn>
</template>

<template>
  <!-- Skips contrast correction; the declared colour is applied as-is -->
  <origam-btn v-contrast="false" color="primary">
    No auto-contrast
  </origam-btn>
</template>

directiveArgs.name: value
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Omit or set to true to enable auto-contrast. Pass false to disable contrast enforcement for this element only.

relations.description_fallback: Uses v-contrast internally when color is an intent token.
Applies v-contrast to ensure the label is legible on any coloured surface.

entry.name: v-hover
entry.description_fallback: Toggles a CSS class on an element while the pointer is over it. Supports a callback mode (.callback modifier) and propagation stop (.stop modifier).

directiveArgs.name: value
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Pass false to disable the directive. Pass { class } to specify the CSS class added while the pointer is over the element.

directiveModifiers.name: callback
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: When present, the directive value is treated as a function (isHovered: boolean) => void instead of a class object.

directiveModifiers.name: stop
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: Calls stopPropagation() on the underlying pointer events, preventing parent hover handlers from triggering.

relations.description_fallback: Commonly combined with v-hover to add elevation or transform on hover.

entry.name: v-intersect
entry.description_fallback: Wraps the IntersectionObserver API. Fires a callback each time the element enters or leaves the viewport. Supports .once to disconnect after the first entry and .quiet to skip the initial call on mount.
entry.note_fallback: IntersectionObserver is not available in IE 11 without a polyfill. Origam does not bundle a polyfill — add one via the project if IE 11 support is required.

directiveArgs.name: handler
directiveArgs.type: (isIntersecting: boolean, entries: IntersectionObserverEntry[], observer: IntersectionObserver) => void
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Callback called on each intersection change. Receives the boolean convenience flag, the raw entries array, and the observer instance.

directiveArgs.name: options
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Standard IntersectionObserver constructor options (root, rootMargin, threshold). Only available in the object form of the binding.

directiveModifiers.name: once
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: Disconnects the observer after the element enters the viewport for the first time. Useful for one-shot animations.

directiveModifiers.name: quiet
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: Suppresses the first callback call that IntersectionObserver fires synchronously on setup, so the handler only fires on real scroll-triggered intersections.

relations.description_fallback: Uses v-intersect internally to detect when the sentinel element reaches the viewport and triggers the next-page load.
OrigamImg lazy-loading mode is powered by v-intersect.

entry.name: v-ripple
entry.description_fallback: Applies a Material-style ripple animation emanating from the click position. Supports centered ripples, circular clipping, custom CSS classes and propagation stop.

examples.title_fallback: Centered circle ripple on an icon button

directiveArgs.name: value
directiveArgs.type: boolean | { class?: string; center?: boolean; circle?: boolean }
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Pass false to disable. Pass an object to set a custom class on the ripple element or to override center/circle via the value (equivalent to using modifiers).

directiveModifiers.name: center
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: The ripple always starts from the geometric center of the element instead of the actual click position. Typically used for icon buttons.

directiveModifiers.name: circle
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: Clips the ripple container to a perfect circle. Combine with .center for round icon buttons.

directiveModifiers.name: stop
directiveModifiers.description_fallback: Calls stopPropagation() on the pointer event after spawning the ripple, preventing parent click handlers from firing.

relations.description_fallback: OrigamBtn uses v-ripple by default (ripple prop). Pass :ripple="false" to disable.
OrigamListItem applies v-ripple on interactive list rows.

entry.name: v-touch
entry.description_fallback: Attaches touch event listeners (touchstart, touchend, touchmove) and recognises swipe gestures (left, right, up, down). All handlers are optional; only attach what you need.
entry.signature:
v-touch="{
  start?:  (e) => void,   // touchstart
  end?:    (e) => void,   // touchend
  move?:   (e) => void,   // touchmove
  left?:   (e) => void,   // swipe left
  right?:  (e) => void,   // swipe right
  up?:     (e) => void,   // swipe up
  down?:   (e) => void,   // swipe down
  parent?: boolean,       // attach listeners to parentElement
  options?: AddEventListenerOptions
}"
entry.note_fallback: The swipe threshold (minimum displacement and velocity) is not currently configurable per-instance. Adjust at the DS level if needed.

kind_extra.signatureSummary: v-touch="{ left, right, up, down, start, end, move }"

directiveArgs.name: start / end / move
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Raw touch lifecycle handlers. The event is augmented with ITouchData (coordinates, delta, velocity, direction).

directiveArgs.name: left / right / up / down
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Swipe direction handlers. Fired when the touchend velocity and displacement exceed the detection threshold.

directiveArgs.name: parent
directiveArgs.description_fallback: When true, listeners are attached to the parentElement instead of the element itself. Useful for scrollable containers where the element may be smaller than the gesture area.

directiveArgs.name: options
directiveArgs.description_fallback: Options forwarded to addEventListener (e.g. { passive: true } to improve scroll performance).

relations.description_fallback: OrigamCarousel uses v-touch to handle swipe navigation between slides.
